reasoning: While the programmatic scores show high textual fidelity (97%+ recall/precision), the LLM fidelity report highlights two major structural and visual failures. Specifically, the loss of content-bearing diagrams and QR codes in a PowerPoint format significantly degrades the document's utility, and the visual duplication of headings indicates a failure in structural mapping. Given the 3x cost penalty for false negatives, the partial disagreement between signals necessitates human intervention.

concerns.analysis: Several meaningful, content-bearing images and diagrams from the source PDF are missing in the HTML output. Specifically, the folder organization diagram (page 9), the photographic image of binders (page 14), the gears collaboration diagram (page 16), the 'DATA' blocks image (page 17), the co-authoring laptop diagram (page 19), and the QR code for follow-up (page 24) are all absent from the rendered HTML.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Diagram of folders (p. 9); photo of colorful binders (p. 14); gears diagram (p. 16); 'DATA' blocks image (p. 17); laptop co-authoring diagram (p. 19); QR code image (p. 24).
concerns.output_quote: The corresponding sections in the HTML (Organization & Structure, Best Practices, Use Cases, Office Applications, and Follow-Up) are missing these visual elements.
concerns.location: Sections: Organization & Structure, Best Practices (OneDrive), Use Cases (SharePoint), Best Practices (SharePoint), Office Applications, and NC DIT Innovation Summit Follow-Up.

concerns.analysis: In the 'Collaboration' and 'Power Automate Flows' sections, sub-headings within the body content are visually duplicated. After the respective content blocks, the headings are listed again as plain text items, which does not reflect the source PDF's design and creates redundant body content.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 8: 'Collaboration' section headings appear once. Page 21: 'Power Automate Flows' section headings appear once.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML displays 'OneDrive Co-authoring', 'Advanced SharePoint Features', and 'Check-in/Check-out Options' as a list at the end of the Collaboration section, following their primary use as headers. Similarly, 'Automating Approvals', 'Custom App Development', and 'Unified Search Experience' are repeated as a list at the end of the Power Automate section.

action_items: Restore missing diagrams and images to the HTML structure with appropriate alt-text
Identify and remove redundant plain-text heading duplicates following the content blocks
Ensure the QR code on page 24 is functional or provided as a link
